cedi: noun. The currency of Ghana, divided into 100 pesewas and represented by ₵.
cent: noun. A subunit of currency equal to one-hundredth of the main unit of currency in many countries.
cite: verb. To quote; to repeat, as a passage from a book, or the words of another.
cited: verb. simple past and past participle of cite
citied: adjective. Containing, or covered with, cities.
den: noun. A small cavern or hollow place in the side of a hill, or among rocks; especially, a cave used by a wild animal for shelter or
dent: noun. A shallow deformation in the surface of an object, produced by an impact.
dentin: noun. The hard, dense calcareous material that makes up the bulk of a tooth.
dice: noun. Gaming with one or more dice.
die: verb. To stop living; to become dead; to undergo death. followed by of as an indication of direct cause; general use:
diet: noun. The food and beverage a person or animal consumes.
din: noun. A loud noise; a cacophony or loud commotion.
dine: verb. To eat; to eat dinner or supper.
dint: noun. Especially in by dint of: force, power.
dit: verb. To stop up; block (an opening); close.
dite: verb. To prepare for use or action; to make ready.
edict: noun. A proclamation of law or other authoritative command.
edit: noun. A change to the text of a document.
end: noun. The terminal point of something in space or time.
etic: adjective. Of or pertaining to analysis of a culture from a perspective situated outside all cultures.
incited: verb. simple past and past participle of incite
indent: noun. A cut or notch in the margin of anything, or a recess like a notch.
indict: verb. To accuse of wrongdoing; charge.
indie: adjective. Independent; from outside the mainstream.
indite: verb. To physically make (letters and words) on a writing surface; to inscribe.
inn: noun. Any establishment where travellers can procure lodging, food, and drink.
inned: verb. simple past and past participle of inn
intend: verb. To fix the mind upon (something, or something to be accomplished); be intent upon
inti: noun. The currency of Peru between 1985 and 1991, replacing the sol.
intine: noun. The innermost wall of a spore or pollen grain
net: noun. A mesh of string, cord or rope.
nine: noun. NATO & ICAO radiotelephony clear code (spelling-alphabet name) for the digit 9.
nit: noun. The egg of a louse.
teiid: adjective. Of or belonging to the Teiidae family of tropical American lizards.
ten: noun. NATO, ICAO, ITU & IMO radiotelephony code for 10, used only with o'clock to indicate direction.
tend: verb. To make a tender of; to offer or tender.
tic: noun. A sudden, nonrhythmic motor movement or vocalization.
tide: noun. The daily fluctuation in the level of the sea caused by the gravitational influence of the moon and the sun.
tin: noun. A malleable, ductile, metallic element, resistant to corrosion, with atomic number 50 and symbol Sn.
tine: noun. A spike or point on an implement or tool, especially a prong of a fork or a tooth of a comb.
tineid: noun. Any moth in the family Tineidae.
tinned: verb. simple past and past participle of tin
cetin: noun. The white, waxy constituent of spermaceti.
cid: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Chimariko.
dei: interjection. Alternative spelling of dey (“informal term of address used when trying to get someone's attention.”).
deni: noun. A subdivision of currency, equal to 0.01 Macedonian denar.
detin: verb. To remove the tin from (metal scrap), usually for resale.
dinic: adjective. Relating to vertigo or dizziness.
ide: noun. A freshwater fish of the family Cyprinidae, found across northern Europe and Asia, especially Leuciscus idus.
incent: verb. To provide an incentive to (a person or organization).
incide: verb. To separate and remove (something); to cut.
indin: noun. A dark red crystalline substance, isomeric with and resembling indigo blue, and obtained from isatide and dioxindol.
itd: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Southern Tidung.
ned: noun. A person, usually a youth, of low social standing and education, a violent disposition and with a particular style of dress
neti: noun. Nasal irrigation performed using a neti pot as part of Hatha yoga.
nid: noun. Alternative form of nide (“nest of pheasants”).
nne: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ngandyera.
tice: noun. A ball bowled to strike the ground about a bat 's length in front of the wicket; a yorker.
ticed: verb. simple past and past participle of tice
tinc: noun. The dyeing poison dart frog (Dendrobates tinctorius).
